detail.point: @SuppressWarnings("resource") is misplaced and suppresses nothing relevant
detail.checked: Base code has no such annotation; this is added by PR. It's a common IntelliJ IDEA annotation for wrapper classes implementing AutoCloseable - harmless regardless

detail.point: Redundant cast (CallableStatement) in wrappedCallableStatement() - VarHandle is already typed correctly from findVarHandle
detail.checked: findVarHandle(CallableStatementWrapper.class, "wrappedStatement", CallableStatement.class) returns VarHandle<CallableStatementWrapper, CallableStatement>, so getAcquire returns CallableStatement directly - cast IS redundant

detail.point: Helper method adds unnecessary indirection; consider calling WRAPPED.getAcquire(this) directly
detail.checked: Style preference - JVM C2 compiler aggressively inlines small methods; helper improves readability vs direct call on every line

detail.point: PR description claims acquire/release avoids "overhead of full volatile semantics" but they ARE semantically equivalent
detail.checked: Verified: volatile read = getAcquire, volatile write = setRelease. If ALL accesses use acquire/release, semantics are identical to volatile. The PR's framing of "avoiding volatile overhead" IS misleading

detail.point: Consider MethodHandles.privateLookupIn() for module boundary safety, though current code is fine
detail.checked: Defensive programming suggestion but candidate admits current MethodHandles.lookup() is fine for same-class access
